additionalType.description: An additional type for the item, typically used for adding more specific types from external vocabularies in microdata syntax. This is a relationship between something and a class that the thing is in. In RDFa syntax, it is better to use the native RDFa syntax - the 'typeof' attribute - for multiple types. Schema.org tools may have only weaker understanding of extra types, in particular those defined externally.
additionalType.title: AdditionalType

identifier.description: The identifier property represents any kind of identifier for any kind of {@link https://schema.org/Thing Thing}, such as ISBNs, GTIN codes, UUIDs etc. Schema.org provides dedicated properties for representing many of these, either as textual strings or as URL (URI) links. See {@link /docs/datamodel.html#identifierBg background notes} for more details.
identifier.title: Identifier

encodingFormat.description: Media type typically expressed using a MIME format (see IANA site and MDN reference)
encodingFormat.title: EncodingFormat

embedUrl.description: A URL pointing to a player for a specific video.
embedUrl.title: EmbedUrl

contentUrl.description: Actual bytes of the media object, for example the image file or video file.
contentUrl.title: ContentUrl

thumbnailUrl.description: A thumbnail image relevant to the Thing
thumbnailUrl.title: ThumbnailUrl

@id.description: IRI identifying the canonical address of this object.
@id.title: @id

disambiguatingDescription.description: A sub property of description. A short description of the item used to disambiguate from other, similar items. Information from other properties (in particular, name) may be necessary for the description to be useful for disambiguation.

enablePrefetch.title: Pré-carregar página do produto
enablePrefetch.description:
Quando ativado, a página do produto começa a carregar assim
que o usuário passa o mouse sobre o card, deixando a navegação mais rápida.
Pode aumentar o consumo de dados em listas muito grandes.
